opportunity_title: CATALYTIC APPLICATION TESTING FOR ACCELERATED LEARNING CHEMISTRIES VIA HIGH-THROUGHPUT EXPERIMENTATION AND MODELING EFFICIENTLY (CATALCHEM-E)
proposed_title: CATALYTIC APPLICATION TESTING FOR ACCELERATED LEARNING CHEMISTRIES VIA HIGH-THROUGHPUT EXPERIMENTATION AND MODELING EFFICIENTLY (CATALCHEM-E): equity focused implementation and evaluation
rationale: This pairing aligns with the opportunity through shared focus on focuses, technology, workflow, improve, management, conditions. Together, the team spans complementary expertise needed to design, implement, and evaluate an equity-centered approach.
aims_or_projects: Aim 1: Quantify need and inequities for focuses using existing datasets and stakeholder input | Aim 2: Adapt and implement an intervention leveraging implementation science and community partnerships | Aim 3: Evaluate effectiveness, reach, and sustainability using pragmatic outcomes and equity metrics
synopsis: This application would address CATALYTIC APPLICATION TESTING FOR ACCELERATED LEARNING CHEMISTRIES VIA HIGH-THROUGHPUT EXPERIMENTATION AND MODELING EFFICIENTLY (CATALCHEM-E) by integrating complementary expertise across the team. The proposed collaboration is centered on program, support, learning, technology and is designed to produce a cohesive, milestone-driven set of deliverables.
rationale_detailed: Faculty were selected for strong topical overlap with the opportunity and complementary, non-redundant strengths. Key contributions: Stephen Wall: Health Equity Research; Valy Fontil: DPP / FQHC / behavior change / blood pressure control / cardiovascular prevention.
suggested_aims: Aim 1: Define and quantify the core problem space in program, support, learning, technology using the team’s established models and datasets.
Aim 2: Develop and optimize an integrated approach that leverages each investigator’s strengths to address the opportunity requirements.
Aim 3: Validate outcomes in the most relevant system and deliver a clear translational or mechanistic endpoint aligned to the FOA.
